scheme_name: Scheme for Assistance to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME): Financial Support to MSMEs in ZED Certification
page_title: Scheme for Assistance to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME): Financial Support to MSMEs in ZED Certification

details: The scheme "Scheme for Assistance to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME)" is an umbrella scheme launched by the Industries and Mines Department, Gujarat. It aims to empower the state's dynamic MSME sector, which significantly contributes to employment, GDP, and exports. Effective from August 7th, 2020 to August 6th, 2025, this initiative supports entrepreneurs and strengthens MSMEs, making them globally competitive.

The component "Financial Support to MSMEs in ZED Certification" scheme promotes Zero Defect and Zero Effect (ZED) manufacturing among Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs). It aims to enhance product quality, improve energy efficiency, and minimize environmental impact.
benefits: MSMEs receive a subsidy of 50% of certification charges, up to a maximum of ₹50,000/-.
Encourages MSMEs to improve product quality and adopt advanced manufacturing techniques.
Promotes the use of energy-efficient manufacturing processes.
eligibility: The firm must be a Micro/Small/Medium enterprise.
The enterprise must be in the manufacturing sector.
The MSME should apply for ZED certification under the scheme.
The MSME must comply with government environmental and quality regulations.

online: portal. Click on "New Investor Registration" to access the registration form.
Fill in the Registration Form. Click on "Submit".
Verify the E-mail ID using the link sent on your registered E-mail ID.
After verification, you will be registered successfully.
Enter the email address and password you used during registration.
Click the "Login" button to access your account.
Now fill in the required details and mandatory documents about your business to complete the application process.

documents_required: Copy of the Constitution of the Enterprise.
CA Certificate for Fixed Capital Investment.
Copy of Self-Certified Shareholding Pattern (if applicable).
CA-Certified Detailed Expenditure Statement for Obtaining ZED Certification.
Undertaking for Any Assistance from GoG and GoI.
Copy of the Bill of Expenditure Incurred.
Copy of the Highlighted Bank Statement.
Copy of GPCB Approved NOC/Consent & Other Department Approvals (if applicable).
Copy of PAN Card of the Enterprise, Promoters/Partners/Directors, and Authorized Signatory.
Copy of CA-Certified Balance Sheet of the Last Financial Year/Annual Audit Report.
Copy of the Last Electricity Bill.
Copy of Testing Equipment Purchased Booklet (if applicable).
Self-Attested Documents/Details Submitted by the Authorized Person.
Power of Attorney/Board Resolution Letter/Consent Letter.

faqs.question: What is the "Scheme for Assistance to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME)"?
faqs.answer: The "Scheme for Assistance to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME)" aims to position Gujarat as a global business destination by promoting sustainable manufacturing, inclusive development, and balanced regional growth.

faqs.question: What is the duration of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme is effective from August 7, 2020, and will remain in operation until August 6, 2025.

faqs.question: What is the objective of the component "Financial Support to MSMEs in ZED Certification"?
faqs.answer: The component "Financial Support to MSMEs in ZED Certification" aims to provide financial assistance to Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) for obtaining Zero Defect and Zero Effect (ZED) certification.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for financial support under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Any MSME registered under the Udyam Registration Portal and engaged in manufacturing or relevant service activities is eligible to apply for financial support under the scheme.

faqs.question: What percentage of financial assistance is provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides 50% subsidy on the ZED certification charges, with a maximum limit of ₹50,000 after deducting other government assistance.

faqs.question: Can an MSME that has already received ZED financial assistance apply again?
faqs.answer: No, an MSME that has already received financial assistance under this scheme is not eligible to apply again.

faqs.question: Can a service-based MSME apply for ZED certification under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, a service-based MSME can apply for ZED certification if it is engaged in activities related to manufacturing and meets the eligibility criteria.

faqs.question: What happens if an MSME fails the ZED certification assessment?
faqs.answer: If an MSME fails the ZED assessment, it will not receive the certification or financial assistance but can make improvements and reapply.

faqs.question: Does ZED certification require renewal?
faqs.answer: Yes, ZED certification may require renewal after a certain period, depending on the scheme’s guidelines and industry standards.

faqs.question: Is training provided to MSMEs under the ZED scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the ZED scheme includes training programs and workshops to help MSMEs implement quality and energy-efficient manufacturing practices.
